title: Aloe vera soothing gel
subtitle: A simple gel made from pure aloe vera juice thickened with xanthan gum for calming irritated skin
excerpt: Pure aloe vera juice thickened to a light gel with xanthan gum. This preparation is suitable for applying to mildly irritated or sun-stressed skin, insect bites, or minor heat exposure. It contains water, so a broad-spectrum preservative is essential. Use within three months.
sourceNotes: Aloe vera gel (Aloe barbadensis leaf juice) in cosmetic preparations: EU Cosmetics Regulation Annex VI, aloe vera safety assessment. Xanthan gum as a cosmetic thickener: standard formulation reference. Preservative requirement for water-containing cosmetics: EU Cosmetics Regulation (EC) No 1223/2009. Optiphen (phenoxyethanol/caprylyl glycol blend) at 1% is COSMOS-approved and effective at pH 4 to 8.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. The xanthan gum percentage (0.5%) scales linearly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: This gel contains water and must include a broad-spectrum preservative. Allow 24 hours for the xanthan gum to fully hydrate before use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Three months with a broad-spectrum preservative at room temperature. Refrigerate if not using daily; refrigeration extends usable life to about four months. Discard if the gel develops cloudiness, separation, an unusual smell, or visible growth.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preservative effective against bacteria, yeast, and mould. Required in any cosmetic preparation that contains water. Without one, a water-based gel may grow visible contamination within days.

content.text: This gel is quick to make. One step cannot be skipped: the
. Any water-based product without one will grow bacteria or mould within days. Optiphen works well at 1%, needs no heating, and suits most skin types.

items.prepNote: pure aloe vera juice, not gel; 97% of batch
0.5% of batch; add slowly while stirring to avoid lumps
1% of batch; broad-spectrum preservative; add last
about 0.25 ml for fragrance; add with the preservative

content.text: Weigh the aloe vera juice into a blender jug or a tall container. With the blender or a hand blender running, slowly sprinkle in the xanthan gum powder in a thin stream. Xanthan gum forms lumps if added all at once. Blend for 30 seconds. The mixture will thicken noticeably.

Add the Optiphen preservative (and the essential oil if using) and stir gently to incorporate. Do not blend at high speed after adding the preservative; excessive aeration introduces bubbles that take days to settle.

Transfer to a clean pump or squeeze bottle. Leave undisturbed for 24 hours; the xanthan gum continues to hydrate and the gel reaches its final consistency. If the gel has air bubbles, leave the cap off for an hour before closing.
Apply a small amount to the inner forearm and wait 24 to 48 hours before applying to the face or larger areas. This is standard practice for any new cosmetic preparation, including water-based gels.

Keep in a cool dark cupboard. Use within three months. Refrigerate between uses if possible; this extends the shelf life and the cold gel gives a pleasant sensation on warm or irritated skin. Discard if the gel becomes cloudy, separates, or develops an off smell.
